tweets.text: RT @AutismCapital: 🚨FYI: Gavin Newsom has already signed California Assembly Bill 1043 which mandates that operating systems including Wind…

retweet.text:
🚨FYI: Gavin Newsom has already signed California Assembly Bill 1043 which mandates that operating systems including Windows, Linux, macOS, and FreeBSD implement system-level age (identity) verification.

This takes effect on January 1st, 2027.

Colorado also has a similar act called SB26-051 which is currently advancing through the committees and ready for full Senate consideration. It would come into effect on January 1st, 2028 if passed.

Utah, Texas, Louisiana, and Alabama all currently have app layer verification laws, but not OS level YET. 

So OS level verification isn't a future thing. It's happening now. They are chokepointing the internet.

Enjoy these days.

quoted.text: It can always get worse, Anon. Heed these warnings. Resist as much as possible. This is bipartisan. Everyone deserves their right to privacy. Whether you want to say F ICE or call your homie a homo online — whatever side you’re on, you must fight this. Pay attention. https://t.co/GmazM3GDF7

retweet.text:
Ten men, every single one of Pakistani migrant background, were convicted today in a British court for attempting to murder a mother and her 13-year-old daughter by petrol-bombing their Stoke-on-Trent home in 2021.

The gang demanded one man, then doused the house in petrol and set the mother on fire in front of her child. The 31-year-old mother suffered 65% burns and was in a two-month coma. The 13-year old girl suffered 15% burns. Both forced to jump from a first-floor window.

This wasn’t “youths” or “Asians”. The perpetrators were identified as Shakil Uddin, Lukman Khan, Riyadh Iqbal, Muhammad Sufyaan-Shah, Tayyeb Majeed, Ali Abbas, Haris Mahmood, Naveed Hussain, Kashif & Bilal Ahmed.

That's the most important energy chart you'll see today.

SpaceX is bringing Dragon home Thursday, February 26

After 185 days docked to the ISS, the CRS-33 spacecraft will undock at 12:05 PM EST, with a splashdown off the California coast expected just before midnight

It’s carrying over 4,000 lbs of wild cargo: frozen stem cells for ALS and Parkinson's research, materials 3D-printed in zero gravity, and Bioprinted liver tissue & blood vessels for organ transplants

These breakthroughs could completely revolutionize healthcare on Earth and tech for upcoming missions
